title: Creamy French Almond Tart
description: A traditional Normandy dessert featuring ground almonds and a caramelized flambe, with a briny and chewy texture, and tamasic-friendly ingredients according to Ayurvedic principles.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the ground almonds, granulated sugar, and fleur de sel.
In a large bowl, whisk together the melted butter, eggs, and vanilla extract.
Add the almond mixture to the butter mixture and stir until well combined.
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a small saucepan, combine the heavy cream and Grand Marnier, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Flambe the cream mixture by igniting it with a match and letting it burn for about 30 seconds.
Pour the flambeed cream mixture over the tart and smooth the top.
Bake the tart for 35-40 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Allow the tart to cool completely on a wire rack.
Just before serving, dust the tart with confectioners sugar.
